description: Manual evaluation scenarios for Mozi operator PRD, PRD review, and SPEC skills.

scenarios.name: create-final-prd-from-complete-brief
scenarios.query: $mozi:create-prd Generate a PRD for AddRelu. NPU ARCH is Mozi NPU v1. The operator computes relu(x + bias) elementwise for float16 and float32 tensors with NumPy-style broadcasting. Inputs are x tensor and bias tensor or scalar. Output has broadcast shape and same dtype as promoted input. Empty tensors follow broadcasting rules. Non-floating dtypes are unsupported. Acceptance must cover normal broadcast, scalar bias, empty tensor, and unsupported dtype.
expected_behavior: Creates docs/mozi/add-relu/prd.md only if the target does not exist or overwrite was explicitly requested.
Uses canonical PRD headings and PyTorch ATen IR prototype.
Writes None for Open Questions when all required information is supplied.
Runs validate_prd.py manually before reporting success.

scenarios.name: clarify-before-final-prd-when-required-facts-missing
expected_behavior: Does not write a final PRD containing TBD or unresolved open questions.
Asks for missing operator behavior, inputs/outputs, NPU ARCH scope, dtype/shape support, and acceptance criteria.
Does not report success until a final PRD passes validate_prd.py.

scenarios.name: revise-existing-prd-from-review-feedback
expected_behavior: Uses revision mode and modifies the provided PRD path only.
Applies PRD-level review feedback without adding SPEC or implementation details.
Asks for clarification if review feedback requires facts not present in the review.
Runs validate_prd.py manually on the modified PRD path before reporting success.

scenarios.name: review-absolute-prd-path
expected_behavior: Reads exactly one absolute PRD path.
Loads rubric.md and output-contract.md.
Outputs YAML only with review_result.prd_path set to the same absolute path.
Validates the YAML with validate_review_yaml.py before final output.

scenarios.name: review-relative-prd-path
expected_behavior: Resolves the relative path against the target repository root.
Outputs YAML only with review_result.prd_path set to the resolved absolute path.
Does not fail solely because the user supplied a relative path.
Validates the YAML with validate_review_yaml.py using the resolved absolute path.

scenarios.name: review-missing-or-multiple-path-failure
expected_behavior: Returns the normal YAML schema with all scores set to 0.
Sets rating to Failed and spec_entry_decision.allowed to false.
Includes a clear blocking issue explaining that exactly one PRD path is required.
Outputs YAML only and validates the failure YAML.

scenarios.name: review-relative-spec-path
expected_behavior: Resolves the relative path against the target repository root.
Outputs YAML only with review_result.spec_path set to the resolved absolute path.
Scores the mandatory Mozi SPEC structures including ATen, Python, C++, InferShape, and InferDtype.
Validates the YAML with validate_review_yaml.py using the resolved absolute path.

scenarios.name: review-invalid-spec-path-failure
expected_behavior: Returns the error YAML schema with total_score set to 0.
Sets error.type to file_not_found and spec_path to the raw provided path.
Includes at least one critical issue and recommended action.
Outputs YAML only and validates the error YAML.

scenarios.name: create-final-spec-from-prd
expected_behavior: Reads the PRD as source of truth and creates the sibling spec.md only if no overwrite confirmation is required.
Uses canonical SPEC headings and required interface, math, shape, and dtype sections.
Writes Acceptance Criteria with all six required numerical-analysis H4 subsections, YAML precision standards, and the exact NumPy compare function signature.
Writes None for Open Issues when all required information is supplied.
Runs validate_spec.py manually before reporting success.

scenarios.name: existing-spec-overwrite-guard
expected_behavior: Detects that the target SPEC already exists.
Asks for confirmation before replacing it because overwrite was not explicitly requested.
Does not edit the existing SPEC before confirmation.

scenarios.name: revise-existing-spec-from-review-feedback
expected_behavior: Uses revision mode and modifies the provided SPEC path only.
Preserves the PRD as requirement source of truth.
Applies only SPEC-level review feedback.
Preserves or restores Acceptance Criteria subsections, all six required numerical-analysis H4 subsections, scenario-to-output YAML precision standards, and the exact NumPy compare function signature.
Runs validate_spec.py manually on the modified SPEC before reporting success.
